questions.question: Define binary heap and explain the difference between max-heap and min-heap with examples.
keyPoints: Binary heap is a complete binary tree with heap property
Max-heap: parent ≥ children, root has maximum element
Min-heap: parent ≤ children, root has minimum element
Example: Max-heap [50,30,40,10,20,35]
Example: Min-heap [10,20,15,25,30]

questions.question: Explain the heapify operation with an example. What is its time complexity?
keyPoints: Heapify maintains heap property by comparing node with children
If child > parent (max-heap), swap and recurse downward
Example showing 3-element swap process
Time complexity: O(log n)
Because height of heap is O(log n)

questions.question: Describe the algorithm to build a max-heap from an unsorted array. Analyze its time complexity.
keyPoints: Start from last non-leaf node: index floor(n/2) - 1
Call heapify on each node in reverse order (bottom-up)
Heapify each subtree to maintain heap property
Time complexity: O(n)
Proof: Sum of heights of all nodes gives O(n)

questions.question: Write the heapsort algorithm and explain how it achieves O(n log n) worst-case complexity.
keyPoints: Step 1: Build max-heap from array - O(n)
Step 2: Repeat n-1 times: swap root with last element, reduce heap size, heapify root
Each extraction takes O(log n), done n times
Total: O(n) + n × O(log n) = O(n log n)
Worst-case = best-case = average-case = O(n log n)

questions.question: Explain why heapsort is preferred over quicksort in real-time systems where worst-case performance matters.
keyPoints: Quicksort has O(n²) worst-case with poor pivot selection
Heapsort guarantees O(n log n) regardless of input
Heapsort is in-place with O(1) auxiliary space
Heapsort has predictable behavior
Quicksort requires random access and is not stable

questions.question: Given array [4, 1, 3, 2, 16, 9, 10, 14, 8, 7], show step-by-step how max-heap is built.
keyPoints: Start from index floor(10/2)-1 = 4
Index 2: node 3, children 10,7 → no swap needed
Index 0: node 4, children 16,14 → swap 4,16 → heapify: node 4 with children 14,9 → swap 4,14 → heapify: node 4 with children 8,9 → swap 4,9 → Final: [16,14,10,8,4,3,9,2,7,1]

questions.question: Write the array representation of a binary heap and derive formulas for finding parent, left child, and right child of any index i.
keyPoints: Array starts from index 0
Parent(i) = floor((i-1)/2)
Left child(i) = 2i + 1
Right child(i) = 2i + 2
Example: i=3 → parent=1, left=7, right=8
